prs.title: Implement MongoDB lock and unlock functionality with error handling
prs.body:
**Description:**

MongoDB migration locking enhancements:

* Added a new lock collection (`mongoLockCollection`) and lock document ID (`mongoLockDocumentID`) for managing migration locks in MongoDB.
* Implemented `lock`, `unlock`, and periodic lock refresh logic in `mongoMigrator` to handle lock acquisition, release, and keepalive, including stale lock cleanup and retry on duplicate key errors.
* Updated migration table initialisation to also create the lock collection, handling "already exists" errors gracefully.

Testing improvements:

* Added unit tests for lock acquisition, release, error handling, and retry logic in `mongo_test.go`, covering scenarios like successful locking, duplicate key errors, and lock release failures.
* Expanded test setup and error definitions to support new lock-related tests, including context handling and mock error values.

fixes: #3149

prs.body: ## Description This PR adds the Web Metrics Raycast extension, which analyses website performance using the Google PageSpeed Insights API. What it includes: - A command to check any URL with a mobile or Desktop strategy - Lighthouse category scores (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO) - Core Web Vitals and key timing metrics (FCP, LCP, CLS, TTFB, TTI, TBT, Speed Index, INP) - Resource breakdown, opportunities, diagnostics, and page weight insights - Local URL history for quick retesting - Action to open the full PageSpeed report and copy a result summary - Secure API key preference setup for Google API access ## Screencast ## Checklist - [x] I read the [extension guidelines](https://developers.raycast.com/basics/prepare-an-extension-for-store) - [x] I read the [documentation about publishing](https://developers.raycast.com/basics/publish-an-extension) - [x] I ran `npm run build` and [tested this distribution build in Raycast](https://developers.raycast.com/basics/prepare-an-extension-for-store#metadata-and-configuration) - [x] I checked that files in the `assets` folder are used by the extension itself - [x] I checked that assets used by the `README` are placed outside of the `metadata` folder

prs.title: Refactor Gemini AI integration to Groq SDK and update configurations
prs.body:
This pull request updates the application's AI categorisation service by switching from Google Gemini to Groq, including all necessary configuration, documentation, and dependency changes. The main changes involve replacing Gemini-specific code, environment variables, and documentation with Groq equivalents.

**AI Service Migration**

* Replaced the Gemini AI service integration in `geminiService.js` with Groq, updating the initialisation and model usage to use `groq-sdk` and the `"llama-3.3-70b-versatile"` model.
* Updated the AI categorisation logic in `geminiService.js` to use Groq's chat completion API, adapting the prompt and response handling to Groq's format.

**Configuration and Documentation Updates**

* Changed `.env.example` to remove `GEMINI_API_KEY` and add `GROQ_API_KEY` for Groq configuration.
* Updated all references in `README.md` from Gemini to Groq, including environment variable instructions and documentation tables. 

**Dependency Management**

* Removed the `@google/generative-ai` package and added the `groq-sdk` dependency in `package.json`.

**Image Preprocessing Refactor:**

**Code Formatting and Consistency:**

prs.title: Implement core ML infrastructure with ResNet50 model and scripts
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a new set of scripts for managing image data and machine learning models, along with updates to the `package.json` to expose these utilities as npm scripts. The changes streamline the workflow for downloading pre-trained models, generating image thumbnails, and producing embeddings for visual search, making it easier to set up and maintain the product catalogue's ML infrastructure.

**New ML and data management scripts:**

* Added `ml/scripts/download_model.ts` to automate downloading and extracting the ResNet-50 model from Kaggle, providing progress feedback and post-download summary.
* Added `ml/scripts/buildIndex.ts` to generate 200×200 thumbnails for all images in the catalogue, skipping already existing thumbnails and reporting summary statistics.
* Added `ml/scripts/generateEmbeddings.ts` to batch-process images, generate normalised embeddings using ResNet-50, store them in a SQLite database, and build a HNSWLib index for fast similarity search. Includes progress bars and summary output.

**Build system and workflow integration:**

* Updated `package.json` to add npm scripts for running the new ML utilities: `download-model`, `generate-embeddings`, and `generate-thumbnails`, pointing to their respective script files.

prs.title: feat: initialize visual product comparison engine with Electron
prs.body:
This pull request sets up the initial foundation for a desktop application using Electron, React, Vite, and Tailwind CSS. It introduces configuration files, dependencies, and starter code for both the main and renderer processes, along with linting and TypeScript support. The most important changes are grouped below by theme.

**Project Setup and Configuration**

* Added `package.json` with dependencies for Electron, React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and supporting libraries, along with scripts for development, build, linting, and model download.
* Added Vite configuration in `vite.config.ts` to enable React, Tailwind, and Electron integration, specifying the Electron main process entry.
* Added TypeScript configuration files for both app (`tsconfig.app.json`) and node (`tsconfig.node.json`) environments, enabling strict type checking and bundler support. 

**Electron Main Process**

* Created `electron/main.ts` to initialize the Electron app, create the browser window, and handle loading either the Vite dev server or production build files.

**Frontend Boilerplate**

* Added React starter files: `index.html` for the root HTML, `src/main.tsx` for React entry point, `src/App.tsx` as a simple interactive component, and basic Tailwind CSS imports in `src/App.css` and `src/index.css`. 
**Linting and Code Quality**

* Introduced modern ESLint configuration in `eslint.config.js` with recommended rules for JavaScript, TypeScript, React Hooks, and Vite-specific plugins, plus global ignores for the `dist` folder.

prs.title: refactor: update GeminiCategorizationService for improved rate limiting and categorization logic
prs.body:
This pull request introduces significant improvements to the `GeminiCategorizationService` in `server/services/geminiService.js`, focusing on more robust AI-based web activity categorisation, enhanced rate limiting and fallback handling, and improved code quality. The categorisation prompt has been rewritten for clarity and precision, new rate-limiting logic and statistics tracking have been added, and the fallback logic and code style have been refined.

**Major improvements and new features:**

*AI Categorisation Enhancements:*
- The prompt for Gemini AI has been rewritten to provide clearer instructions, more detailed classification guidelines, explicit examples, and a stricter response format, resulting in more accurate and consistent categorisations.

*Rate Limiting, Caching, and Resilience:*
- Added advanced rate-limiting logic, including request spacing, exponential backoff with retries, and a fallback mechanism when the API is unavailable or rate-limited. A domain-based cache is now used to speed up repeated categorisations for the same domain. 

*Fallback and Pattern Matching Improvements:*
- Expanded and cleaned up the fallback domain pattern lists for all categories, and improved code style for pattern matching and tag processing. 

*General Code Quality:*
- Improved code style and consistency throughout, including use of arrow functions, trailing commas, and consistent formatting in array and object literals.

*Dependency Update:*
- Updated the `caniuse-lite` dependency in `client/package-lock.json` to the latest version for security and compatibility.

prs.title: feat: docker postgres setup
prs.body:
This pull request introduces significant improvements to the development and deployment workflow, primarily by migrating the backend from SQLite to PostgreSQL, enhancing environment variable management, and improving CI/CD processes. These changes make the project more suitable for production use and streamline local development and automated testing.

**Database Migration and Environment Management:**

* Migrated the backend database from SQLite to PostgreSQL in `server/config/settings.py`, with connection parameters now loaded from environment variables using `python-dotenv`, allowing for more flexible and secure configuration.
* Added `psycopg2-binary` and `python-dotenv` to `server/pyproject.toml` to support PostgreSQL and environment variable loading, respectively.

**Development and Deployment Workflow:**

* Added a `server/docker-compose.yml` file to provide an easy way to run a local PostgreSQL database using Docker, including persistent volumes and health checks.

**Continuous Integration Enhancements:**

* Updated `.github/workflows/server-ci.yml` to split linting and testing into separate jobs, ensure linting runs before tests, and set up a PostgreSQL service for tests. Environment variables are configured for the test database, and the workflow now also runs migrations before tests.

prs.title: docs: update README to reflect Django backend migration
prs.body:
This pull request updates the `README.md` to reflect a migration of the backend from a Node.js/Express/Passport stack to a Python/Django/Django REST Framework stack. The instructions for setup, environment variables, and development have all been revised to match the new backend architecture, including the use of `uv` for Python dependency management. The frontend (React/Vite) remains unchanged.

**Backend stack migration and documentation updates:**

* Updated backend stack in the "Backend" section from Node.js/Express/Passport to Python 3.11+, Django 5.2.8, Django REST Framework 3.16.1, and related Python tools (`uv`, `Pillow`).
* Replaced Node.js/Express-related environment variables and setup instructions with Django-specific settings, including `SECRET_KEY`, `ALLOWED_HOSTS`, and an optional `DATABASE_URL`.
* Updated backend installation and running instructions to use Python and `uv` instead of Node.js and npm, including new commands for dependency installation and server startup.
* Revised backend development workflow instructions to reflect Django conventions, such as running migrations and creating a superuser, and removed references to Express, nodemon, and API endpoint setup in `index.js`.

**Frontend and general documentation:**

* Clarified frontend installation steps and removed outdated production build/preview instructions, focusing the documentation on development setup.

prs.title: refactor: update Dashboard and AppList components
prs.body:
This pull request enhances the `AppList` component to support category-based filtering and sorting, introduces a compact display mode for improved dashboard integration, and refines the filtering and sorting UI for better usability. These changes make it easier for users to filter activities by category and productivity level, and to view recent updates in a more concise format.

**Filtering and Sorting Enhancements:**
* Added category filtering and sorting to `AppList`, allowing users to filter activities by category and sort by category name. 
* Refactored productivity filtering UI and improved filter/sort dropdowns for clarity and consistency. 
**Compact Mode and Dashboard Integration:**
* Introduced a `compact` prop to `AppList`, which streamlines the UI for dashboard usage by hiding the search bar and sort dropdown, and displaying a "Latest Updates" label. 
**Performance and Data Handling:**
* Updated memoisation dependencies and filtering logic to ensure category filters are applied efficiently and correctly. [
**UI and Layout Improvements:**
* Improved layout and grouping of filter/sort controls, and updated results count and activity list rendering for clarity. 

These updates make the activity list more flexible and user-friendly, especially when embedded in the dashboard.

prs.title: refactor: update Dashboard and AppList components
prs.body:
This pull request enhances the `AppList` component to support more flexible filtering and sorting, improves its usability in compact mode, and updates its integration in the dashboard. The most significant changes include the addition of category-based filtering, support for a compact display mode, and an expansion of sorting options.

**AppList component improvements:**

* Added a category filter dropdown that allows users to filter activities by their `categoryName`, with the list of categories dynamically generated from the data. 
* Introduced a `compact` prop: in compact mode, the search bar and some controls are hidden, and a simplified header is shown instead. 
* Expanded sorting options to include sorting by `categoryName` and updated the sorting logic accordingly.
* Refactored the filter and sort controls for clarity and better grouping, including always showing the category filter and updating productivity filter labels. 

**Dashboard integration:**

* Updated the Dashboard to use the new `compact` mode for the recent activity summary, increased the height of the summary panel, and adjusted the number and sort order of displayed items.

prs.title: CI: add server CI workflow for pull requests
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a new GitHub Actions workflow for continuous integration (CI) of the server component and makes minor updates to the client’s `package-lock.json` file. The workflow ensures server code is linted and tested on pull requests, while the lockfile changes remove unnecessary `peer` fields from several dependencies.

The most important changes are:

**Server CI Workflow:**

* Added a new GitHub Actions workflow in `.github/workflows/server-ci.yml` to automatically lint, check migrations, and run tests for the server code on pull requests targeting the `main` branch. This helps catch issues early and maintain code quality.

**Client Dependency Updates:**

* Removed the `"peer": true` property from multiple dependencies in `client/package-lock.json`, cleaning up the lockfile and preventing potential confusion about peer dependency requirements. prs.title: feat: Implement initial user and club applications with models, API views, serializers, admin, and configure DRF, CORS, and project settings.
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a comprehensive backend foundation for the NST-SDC Portal, including custom user management, club activity tracking, and REST API endpoints. The main changes are the implementation of a custom user model, models for club activities (projects, events, attendance, tasks), REST API views and serializers for these entities, admin configurations, and essential settings for authentication, media, and CORS.

**User Management and Authentication**
* Implemented a custom `User` model in `users/models.py` with extended profile, academic, club, and OAuth fields, including points for gamification, skill levels, and social links.
* Added a custom admin interface for the `User` model for detailed management in `users/admin.py`.
* Created a serializer and profile API endpoint for authenticated user data in `users/serializers.py`, `users/views.py`, and `users/urls.py`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a96900f1d79dd88262bd75e422c800dbe0c4f1fb06c8d1150848bf0cbfb0b24dR1-R16) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ce8c6bcbaeedb564606e4ae6ef6caeb1dbbd4655b6cdbaeebd7449bc23d65855R1-R11) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0a5682efeaae04b54747f52b8bc4259a58e9c8ca38bd797ee7e4d6f8abacf1bcR1-R6)

**Club Activity Models and Admin**
* Added models for `Project`, `Event`, `Attendance`, and `Task` in `club/models.py` to track club projects, events, member attendance, and assigned tasks.
* Registered these models with custom admin configurations for effective management in `club/admin.py`.

**REST API Endpoints and Serialization**
* Implemented serializers for club models and a dashboard API endpoint to fetch user-specific active tasks and upcoming events in `club/serializers.py`, `club/views.py`, and `club/urls.py`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3ed5db9bfd9e3de4ccfe6d085c62afea1b070b4615c5863f2d7c8b143420ae3eR1-R25) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e080a5dac6a5020aaae2207700da23e9e3fcb904c471ad8f0939606d92f6615eR1-R31) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-e0616990eebdff6cdab21cd27607e232df56df4766ee01dcc5551f5ca8a1dfe8R1-R12)

prs.title: bug: fix config
prs.body:
This pull request introduces improvements to environment variable handling and API endpoint configuration for both the client and server, as well as refines CORS settings for better security and flexibility. The changes ensure that the application can seamlessly switch between local development and production environments and that API calls are correctly routed.

**Environment variable and API configuration:**

* The `API_BASE_URL` is now set to use `import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL` if available, or fallback to `'http://localhost:3000'` for local development in `client/src/config.js`.
* The Astro/Vite config (`client/astro.config.mjs`) now defines `import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL` at build time, defaulting to the production API URL if not set in the environment.
* The homepage (`client/src/pages/index.astro`) imports `API_BASE_URL` and injects it into client-side scripts, ensuring all API calls use the correct base URL. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-3a5301099c5da12c0ff9e1ce4e88b2785f6f4a999a6c3592ed8a37df69fc37ceR14) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3a5301099c5da12c0ff9e1ce4e88b2785f6f4a999a6c3592ed8a37df69fc37ceL52-R58)

**Server-side CORS improvements:**

* The server (`server/server.js`) now supports a whitelist of allowed origins, including localhost, production, and staging URLs, improving security by only allowing requests from trusted origins. The CORS middleware dynamically checks the incoming request's origin against this list.

prs.title: deploy: fixes for deployment
prs.body:
This pull request implements significant improvements to authentication handling across both the client and server, especially for OAuth and token management. It enhances security and user experience by ensuring tokens are consistently managed via cookies and Authorization headers, and by improving compatibility with different environments (production vs. development). Additionally, there are adjustments to deployment configuration and some cleanup of development files.

**Authentication and Token Handling Improvements**

* Refactored both `isAuthenticated` and `isAuthenticatedTemp` middleware to accept tokens from either cookies or the `Authorization` header, enabling support for clients that store tokens in cookies or send them as Bearer tokens. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bbb07e34f1786b5b5d2a64c714b7fcd838b2486b3f70f78100438896979b3319L57-R64) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-bbb07e34f1786b5b5d2a64c714b7fcd838b2486b3f70f78100438896979b3319L90-R104)
* Added a request interceptor in the client’s `api.js` to automatically include the token from cookies as a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header for all outgoing API requests.
* Updated the logout logic on the client to explicitly clear the token cookie and redirect to the home page, ensuring tokens are removed from the browser.

**OAuth and Profile Completion Flow**

* Improved handling of OAuth callback redirects: the server now always redirects to the client with the token as a URL parameter, and the client sets the token in a cookie and cleans up the URL. This ensures seamless login and profile completion for OAuth users. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43R146) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L162-R158) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L180-R170) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-56967fbff512421cec573aac54bdfa6a7d42f0d2ea8c337d99b77b3d19c106dfR6-R11) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-56967fbff512421cec573aac54bdfa6a7d42f0d2ea8c337d99b77b3d19c106dfR331-R345) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-3519af58a1404c91ff542dd3f6b53bf62dfc67d5f3e022448bb7a976c7784832R10-R15) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-3519af58a1404c91ff542dd3f6b53bf62dfc67d5f3e022448bb7a976c7784832R288-R301)
* After login or profile completion, the client now stores the returned token in a cookie for persistent authentication. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d7cc9d3ea98c0e830e2cd451fe2ec891a03ee7d4ca06ab60902468b94a49d42aR264-R267) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d7cc9d3ea98c0e830e2cd451fe2ec891a03ee7d4ca06ab60902468b94a49d42aR280-R283) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-56967fbff512421cec573aac54bdfa6a7d42f0d2ea8c337d99b77b3d19c106dfR460-R463) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-56967fbff512421cec573aac54bdfa6a7d42f0d2ea8c337d99b77b3d19c106dfR488-R491)

**Security and Environment Compatibility**

* Centralized cookie options in the server to ensure `SameSite`, `secure`, and `httpOnly` flags are set appropriately for production and development environments, improving security and cross-environment compatibility. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43R10-R19) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L24-R34) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L79-R84) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L103-R108) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43L134-R135) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-0d968923d1d8e334fbf75d574dc31da85a0b8701b13ad9283babf8bd5a951f43R187-R191)

**Configuration and Cleanup**

* Switched the Astro deployment adapter from Node to Vercel for serverless deployment, and updated the Tailwind plugin configuration.
* Removed obsolete VSCode workspace configuration files (`extensions.json` and `launch.json`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-99ada1c7c4fc1ca373530410950ffbf21f04276ae1c851f69e3a65fc2b05f127L1-L4) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-fbc3ebfca060786273cd6063a1ab464347d339cff839e45d4fb7755bfcf3b72dL1-L11)
* Updated the API base URL environment variable for the client to use `VITE_API_URL` for better compatibility with Vite.
* Simplified Prisma client logging configuration on the server.

This pull request introduces several improvements and fixes across the frontend and backend, focusing on authentication, user experience, and code consistency. The most notable changes include strengthening route protection for organizers, standardizing sign-up links, enhancing dashboard logic for organizer visibility, and adding session support on the server.

**Authentication and Authorization:**
- Strengthened organizer route protection in `organiser.astro` by decoding the JWT token and redirecting unauthorized users, ensuring only users with the correct role can access organizer pages.

**User Experience and Consistency:**
- Standardized all "register" links to use `/signup` and updated call-to-action buttons and links in `CTA.astro`, `HowItWorks.astro`, and `Stats.astro` for consistency and improved navigation. 
- Improved dashboard sidebar logic to show or hide the organizer link based on the user's role, enhancing the user interface for organizers and attendees.

**Backend and Session Management:**
- Added `express-session` middleware and configuration to the server, enabling session support for user authentication and integrating with Passport.js. 
- Updated the OAuth callback to set the JWT token as an HTTP-only cookie and fixed the redirect URL for profile completion.

**Dependency and Code Cleanup:**
- Removed unused `package.json` from the client directory, cleaning up unnecessary dependencies.

prs.title: Add Pagination
prs.body:
This pull request adds pagination support to the event listing API, allowing clients to request events in pages and receive relevant pagination metadata in the response. The main changes are in both the controller and service layers to handle pagination parameters and return structured pagination info.

**API enhancements:**

* The `getAllEvent` controller now accepts `page` and `limit` query parameters, passing them to the service and including a `pagination` object in the response.

**Backend logic improvements:**

* The `getAllEventService` function now calculates pagination values, fetches only the requested page of events, and returns pagination metadata (such as `currentPage`, `totalPages`, `totalCount`, `limit`, `hasNextPage`, and `hasPrevPage`) along with the events.

prs.title: Add OAuth
prs.body:
**Authentication and OAuth Integration**
* Added new dependencies: `passport`, `passport-google-oauth20`, and their supporting packages (`passport-oauth2`, `passport-strategy`, `oauth`, `base64url`, `pause`, `uid2`, `utils-merge`) to enable Google OAuth authentication in the server.
* Updated the `.env.example` file to include Google OAuth configuration variables (`GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL`) and clarified JWT-related variables.

**Dependency Cleanup**
* Removed `nodemon` and its transitive dependencies (such as `chokidar`, `anymatch`, `ignore-by-default`, `minimatch`, `glob-parent`, `pstree.remy`, `simple-update-notifier`, `supports-colour`, etc.), reducing unnecessary development dependencies. 

These changes prepare the server for Google OAuth authentication and simplify the dependency tree for easier maintenance.

prs.title: Add Event Route
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a new event management feature to the backend, including endpoints for creating, updating, retrieving, and validating events. It also refactors the development workflow and dependencies. The most significant changes are grouped below:

**Event Feature Implementation**

* Added new service layer in `eventServices.js` for handling event creation, retrieval, update, and fetching by ID, using Prisma ORM for database operations.
* Implemented controller functions in `eventControllers.js` to handle HTTP requests for event operations, delegating business logic to the service layer.
* Created middleware in `eventMiddleware.js` for validating event data during creation and update, ensuring data integrity and proper error handling.
* Defined event routes in `eventRoutes.js` to expose RESTful endpoints for event operations, integrating validation and controllers.
* Registered event routes in the main router (`routes/index.js`) under `/api/event`, making the event API accessible.

**Development Workflow and Dependency Updates**

* Updated the `dev` script in `package.json` to use native Node.js file watching (`node --watch`) instead of `nodemon`, and removed the `nodemon` dependency.

prs.title: Add backend with Rust
prs.body:
This pull request introduces the initial implementation of the IRIS server backend in Rust, including setup documentation, environment configuration, and core modules for authentication, database connection, user management, and project/coin/message models. The most important changes are grouped below by theme:

**Project Setup and Documentation**

* Added `Cargo.toml` specifying Rust dependencies (`axum`, `mongodb`, `oauth2`, etc.) for building a web server with GitHub OAuth and MongoDB support.
* Added `SETUP.md` with step-by-step instructions for configuring GitHub OAuth and running the server locally.
* Added `.env.example` with environment variable templates for MongoDB, GitHub OAuth, and JWT secret configuration.

**Authentication and Middleware**

* Implemented GitHub OAuth authentication logic in `src/auth/mod.rs`, including user creation/upsert and JWT token generation on successful login.
* Added JWT authentication and admin authorization middleware in `src/middleware/auth.rs`, including token creation, verification, and request extension for authenticated user info. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-6f077c67ce7b0c0211e73f9e31991b0e3a1ac987965fa0fd8ad5932777118478R1-R195) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-68ed87005f3d8ed3f1b846a7db86e3f0ae76177f84ee16f9def28fe0f4fa1ea9R1-R3)

**Database and Models**

* Defined MongoDB connection logic and application state in `src/db/mod.rs`, supporting collections for users, projects, messages, coins, and leaderboards.
* Implemented core data models for `User`, `Project`, `Message`, `CoinTransaction`, and `WeeklyLeaderboard` in the `src/models` directory, with serialization and MongoDB integration. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-2d79624763ea9cbd5f632368c720fc3f60c4bc4d7bbe772f624f3f78bc566d05R1-R24) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ef08d2baf5cd6874c9b49e501265e46102127912bc80075b88397b513dd59782R1-R23) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-8ad9a1118bbb5b6051796d1cd80110a903b0cc61f2955905efa23151533f05daR1-R15) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-d431c362bf5867ea1bc8b609ddbf510fc9248ef6860b719c3b3175ff01ac315cR1-R31) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-dd0b5157ebbf03b6656be546366ec087f0ee1220ae2ab11544d9370f9234bd77R1-R9)

**Server Entrypoint**

* Added `src/main.rs` to initialize environment, connect to the database, build routes, and start the Axum server on port 5657.

prs.title: feat: add development.clientOverlay option to disable error overlay
prs.body:
Adds a new clientOverlay boolean option under development config for `Bun.serve`, similar to Vite's `client.overlay` option. When set to false, disables the in-browser error overlay in development mode, logging errors only to console.

### What does this PR do?
Adds a `development.clientOverlay` option to `Bun.serve()` that allows disabling the error overlay in development mode (similar to Vite's server.hmr.overlay option).

### How did you verify your code works?
- Add `ServerConfig.client_overlay_in_dev_for_bake `(defaults to true)
- Wire option through `server.zig` -> `DevServer.init` -> `DevServer.Options`
- Pass overlay flag to client bundle metadata via IncrementalGraph
- Update memory cost tracking in `DevServer.deinit` and `memory_cost.zig`

Usage:
```
  Bun.serve({ development: { hmr: true, clientOverlay: false  // disable overlay } })
```

Solves issue #24636

prs.title: feat: Add Prisma schema import support (Beta)
prs.body:
## Overview
Adds support for importing Prisma ORM schema files into DrawDB with automatic SQL export to the detected database provider.

## Changes
- Added Prisma schema import option in File > Import from menu (marked as Beta)
- Implemented `fromPrisma()` parser in `src/utils/importFrom/prisma.js`
- Parse `model` blocks → tables with fields
- Parse `enum` blocks → enums
- Parse `@relation()` → relationships with cardinality inference
- Parse field attributes: `@id`, `@@id([...])`, `@unique`, `@default()`, `autoincrement()`
- Detect `datasource provider` and auto-set diagram database
- Auto-arrange imported tables on canvas
- Reject the MongoDB provider with a clear error message

## Supported Prisma Features
### Models & Fields
- Model declarations → tables
- Field types, nullability (`?`), lists (`[]`)
- Primary keys: `@id` and composite `@@id([field1, field2])`
- Unique constraints: `@unique`
- Default values: `@default(value)` including `autoincrement()`

### Relationships
- `@relation(fields: [...], references: [...])` 
- Cardinality inference (one-to-many for list types, many-to-one otherwise)
- Named relations via `name: \"relationName\"`

### Enums
- Full enum support

### Datasource Detection
Automatically detects and maps the provider to the diagram database:
- \`postgresql\` / \`postgres\` → PostgreSQL
- \`mysql\` → MySQL
- \`mariadb\` → MariaDB
- \`sqlite\` → SQLite
- \`sqlserver\` → SQL Server
- \`cockroachdb\` → PostgreSQL
- \`mongodb\` → rejected (not SQL)

## Usage
1. Open DrawDB editor
2. File → Import from → Prisma schema (Beta)
3. Upload your \`.prisma\` file
4. Tables, relationships, and enums are imported
5. Export to SQL using the detected database provider

## Limitations (Beta)
- Does not parse composite types or views
- Limited support for advanced Prisma features (e.g., named indexes from `@@unique([...])`, `@@index`)
- Does not parse `@map` / `@@map` for custom column/table names
- No `onDelete` / `onUpdate` cascade parsing yet

## Testing
- ESLint passes on all changed files
- Import flow integrated with the existing modal system
- Error handling for malformed schemas
- Database provider detection is working

## Files Changed
- \`src/data/constants.js\` — Added IMPORT_FROM.PRISMA
- \`src/components/EditorHeader/ControlPanel.jsx\` — Added menu option
- \`src/components/EditorHeader/Modal/ImportDiagram.jsx\` — Prisma file handling
- \`src/components/EditorHeader/Modal/Modal.jsx\` — Database auto-setting
- \`src/utils/importFrom/prisma.js\` — New parser implementation

Closes issue #681

prs.title: Feature based folder structure
prs.body:
This pull request refactors the user management functionality in the server-side codebase to use a modular, service-based architecture. It moves user-related controllers, routes, middleware, and services into a dedicated `src/users` directory, introduces validation middleware for user actions, and updates imports and file paths throughout the server. The changes improve code organization, maintainability, and separation of concerns.

**User Management Refactor and Modularization**

_User controller and service separation:_
* Replaced the monolithic `userController.js` with a modular setup: user controller (`userController.js`), validation middleware (`userMiddleware.js`), and service layer (`userServices.js`) in `server/src/users/`. Controllers now delegate business logic to services, improving maintainability and testability. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0d3b482ca271c8c8e79b0785a41684c1f6e414e1939dda52c8e79503f64b1400L1-L230) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c70bac0f40bdf1eea59ebfa7a8ead7219ebc9f0f70736440056eaf4203ce3631R1-R103) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-59be23b596c95b04c2a819694cf43a6f29fd2e4623b210f9e7e2bae1380c2eddR1-R196)

_Route and middleware improvements:_
* Replaced the old `userRoutes.js` with a new version in `server/src/users/` that applies validation middleware for registration, login, update, and delete endpoints, ensuring request data is validated before reaching controllers. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d99b6667544cd7047576e27ca0af16dce7325eca9ed7c27e3762f5452e225863L1-L20) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-120bd475ec53970e180fe633c0e5fc58bac42ee6b7a787048085fedf714a9112R1-R21) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-cea023b35c3fa01e0e9b758c721b186f9aa5f7cc12bfa02f6a8eed1b985b4bb6R1-R54)

_Project structure and import updates:_
* Moved and renamed files for better organization, including moving database config and routes to `src/` and updating all relevant import paths. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-bdac68f53868244c7a564eb9e075d9ddde239be162d4a8c656c78c1c55e51e92L3-R3) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-475aa6062062c0a7a00a6edd49daaa93787a162c9f69eed76048e82bd1267a2dL2-R2) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-8cf1ffe3788af127768748703e2f15dcfb3a05ffd20b4c2375223637e3260938L28-R28)

**Minor Improvements**

* Added `.vscode/` to `.gitignore` to prevent committing editor settings.

**Removed Redundant Code**

* Deleted unused imports and obsolete files from previous structure, including old controllers and routes. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-41ec90e83607b4c83e1d367e38270eb8f38ec4684ff4089827136c3d6f4ded10L1) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b298e0ec086a9a8ce4be16aed2d0b88a9f8f11d38688d433bbdc100105c0e14cL1)

These changes collectively make the user management code more modular, easier to test, and maintain, while improving validation and error handling.

prs.title: Update in command for ER Diagram
prs.body:
This pull request introduces improvements to the developer workflow for managing ER diagrams and updates project configuration files to better support development practices. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Developer Workflow Improvements:**

* Updated the ER diagram instructions in the `README.md` to clarify that `npm run createER` is used to generate the diagram and `npm run showER` is used to view it, replacing the previous single command.
* Added new scripts to `server/package.json` for creating (`createER`) and serving (`showER`) the ER diagram, streamlining the process for developers.

**Project Configuration and Cleanliness:**

* Expanded `.gitignore` in `server/.gitignore` to exclude additional files and directories such as `.DS_Store`, `package-lock.json`, `dist/`, IDE config folders, and Prisma migrations, helping keep the repository clean and preventing accidental commits of local or build artifacts.

This pull request introduces significant updates to the backend data model and documentation for the project. The most important changes are the addition of new Prisma models to support users, events, registrations, and reviews, as well as updates to the documentation to reflect new features.

**Database schema enhancements:**

* Added new Prisma models: `User`, `Event`, `Registration`, and `Review` to support user management, event organization, registration tracking, and event reviews. Each model includes relevant fields and relationships, enabling more complex event management functionality.
* Introduced enums `Role` and `PaymentStatus` to standardize user roles and registration payment states, improving data integrity and query simplicity.

**Documentation updates:**

* Updated `README.md` to include instructions for serving the ER diagram, helping developers visualize the database schema.

prs.title: Build front-end tests
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a comprehensive frontend testing setup for the project, including configuration for CI, test utilities, mock data factories, and a suite of unit and integration tests for key components and API modules. The changes are grouped into three main themes: test infrastructure, mock data and utilities, and new test coverage for frontend modules.

Test infrastructure and configuration:

* Added a new GitHub Actions workflow (`.github/workflows/frontend-tests.yml`) to run frontend tests, linting, and coverage reporting on push and pull request events for relevant branches and paths. This includes matrix testing for Node.js versions and coverage badge generation.
* Updated `frontend/package.json` to add scripts for running tests and coverage with Vitest, and included new dev dependencies for Vitest, Testing Library, Jest, and related tools. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-da6498268e99511d9ba0df3c13e439d10556a812881c9d03955b2ef7c6c1c655L9-R14) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-da6498268e99511d9ba0df3c13e439d10556a812881c9d03955b2ef7c6c1c655R59-R78)
* Created `frontend/jest.setup.js` to configure the test environment, including mocking browser APIs (e.g., `matchMedia`, `IntersectionObserver`, `ResizeObserver`) and suppressing noisy console errors during tests.

Mock data and test utilities:

* Added `frontend/src/__mocks__/mock-data.ts` containing factory functions and example objects for sources and notebooks, enabling robust and reusable test data for API and UI tests.

Frontend component and API test coverage:

* Implemented unit and integration tests for core frontend modules:
  - [`src/lib/utils`](diffhunk://#diff-ecd46d10f3dccba73cbf32208aac3ed9cc4bcec8a93bf6e233bf9fb7dd13d71dR1-R66): Added tests for the className utility function (`cn`).

[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5242ad874e1c22757f1ab4dd88bedbc19aab5405a795f698625fffd395a0887aR1-R54) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-615d88d6f7dbdc2249bc7292f009d06c293327a0955cf30b3b1080cde4fe7c6aR1-R57)

prs.title: Feature/chat room
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a new "Professional Chat" text-only mode alongside the existing video chat functionality. It adds the backend logic for queueing, matching, and managing professional text chat sessions, updates the frontend to allow users to choose between video and text chat modes, and creates a new page for the professional chat lobby.

**Professional Chat (Text-Only) Feature:**

* Added backend support for professional text-only chat, including queue management, user matching, room creation, and partner handling in `UserManager`. New socket event handlers manage joining/leaving the queue, matching, messaging, typing indicators, and session transitions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4R9-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4R33) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4L41-R63) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4L62-R74) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4R282-R405) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-a909ecdb7af68afdeadb4c36b691cbaef52780e7f27bbfb4d06fce35836defe4R482-R508) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-c20200a666b149a045e365999f581db8e04687cfc9569bdf4c9477cab954d324R76-R113)

**Frontend User Flow Updates:**

* Updated the landing page (`frontend/app/page.tsx`) to let users select either "Video Chat" or "Professional Chat" modes, with clear descriptions and navigation.
* Added a new professional chat page (`frontend/app/professional-chat/page.tsx`) that loads the professional chat lobby component.

prs.title: fix issue of routing and add structure
prs.body:
This pull request introduces a foundational backend structure for user management in the GitHub Activity Tracker project. It adds database integration with PostgreSQL (using GORM), an in-memory fallback store, and a clean separation of concerns via model, store, service, and handler layers. The main server logic is updated to initialise the appropriate storage backend and expose a user creation endpoint.

**Database Integration and Configuration**
- Adds `server/database/database.go` to manage PostgreSQL connection setup, configuration from environment variables, connection pooling, and auto-migration of models. Provides helper methods for database access and graceful shutdown.

**User Data Model**
- Defines a `GitId` struct in `server/model/user.go` representing a user with fields for UUID, GitHub ID (unique), and timestamps, compatible with GORM for ORM mapping.

**Store Layer (Persistence Abstraction)**
- Implements the `UserStore` interface in `server/store/user.go` with two concrete stores:
  - `InMemoryStore` for testing or fallback, storing users in a map.
  - `PostgreSQLStore` for persistent storage using GORM and PostgreSQL.
  - Both stores provide methods for CRUD operations on `GitId` records.

**Service and Handler Layers**
- Adds a `UserService` in `server/service/user.go` for business logic, delegating persistence to the store layer and exposing methods for user management.
- Introduces a `UserHandler` in `server/handler/user.go` with an endpoint for adding a GitHub ID, including request validation and error handling.

**Server Initialisation and Routing**
- Updates `server/server.go` to initialise the database (with fallback to in-memory store), wire up dependencies, define the `/user` POST endpoint, and add a health check endpoint. Starts the server using the chosen storage backend.

issues.title: Add migration locks in MongoDB
issues.body:
I would like to propose adding migration locks for MongoDB to ensure that migrations do not run concurrently across multiple instances of an application.

Since MongoDB is a NoSQL database, the locking approach differs from traditional RDBMS implementations. Below is a proposed approach.

1. Use a dedicated lock collection
     - Create a collection (e.g., `migration_locks`) that stores a lock document.
        - Example structure:
            ```go
            {
              "_id": "migration_lock",
              "lockedAt": <timestamp>,
              "lockedBy": <instance-id>
            }
             ```
     - Before running migrations:
        - Attempt to insert or acquire the lock document.
        - If the lock already exists, the migration process should exit or wait.
        - If the lock is acquired successfully, proceed with the migration.

     - After migrations complete:
        - Delete the lock document to release the lock.

2. Use Atomic Operations
     - MongoDB operations such as:
        - `findOneAndUpdate`
        - `insertOne` with a `unique _id`
        can ensure atomic lock acquisition, preventing race conditions.

3. Optional: TTL Index for Safety
     - To avoid stale locks if a migration process crashes, we can add a TTL index on lockedAt.
         - Example: 
            - Automatically expire lock documents after a configurable duration.

4. Transactions (If Available)
     - If MongoDB transactions are available (MongoDB ≥ 4.0 with replica sets):
     - Lock acquisition and migration metadata updates could run within a session transaction.
     - This would improve the reliability and atomicity of migration operations.

## Summary
The API endpoints are currently accessible without authentication, which poses a security risk. To enhance the protection of our API, implement an API key mechanism that restricts access to authorized clients only.

## Proposed Solution
- Introduce API key verification for all sensitive endpoints.
- Store valid API keys securely (e.g., environment variables or a secrets manager).
- Reject requests with missing or invalid API keys with a 401 Unauthorized response.

## Tasks

- [ ] Implement middleware for API key validation
- [ ] Update API documentation to include authentication details
- [ ] Test endpoint access with and without valid API keys

## Impact
This enhancement will greatly improve the security of our API, preventing unauthorized access and potential data leaks.

issues.title: Optimize Database Indexes and Query Performance
issues.body:
### Problem
The Activity model and related aggregation queries in the backend are not fully optimized. Multiple queries lack composite indexes and result caching, leading to potential performance bottlenecks as data scales. Aggregation pipelines in activity analytics endpoints can be improved for speed and efficiency, and database connection pooling is not configured.

### Suggested Improvements
- Add composite indexes to the Activity model for frequently queried fields (userId, domain, startTime, sessionId, category)
- Review and optimize MongoDB aggregation pipelines in activity analytics endpoints
- Implement in-memory caching or Redis for frequently accessed data
- Configure MongoDB connection pooling for backend
- Profile database queries and monitor query performance in production

### Relevant Code Files
- server/models/activity.model.js
- server/controllers/activity.controller.js
- server/routes/activity.route.js
- server/config/db.js

### Acceptance Criteria
- Composite indexes added to Activity and other frequently queried models
- Aggregation pipelines are optimized for speed
- Caching implemented for heavy endpoints
- Connection pooling enabled
- Query performance is monitored

issues.title: Add API Request Timeout and Retry Logic
issues.body:
### Problem
API requests made by the frontend, browser extension, and backend (Gemini service, etc.) lack proper timeout handling and retry logic. This can cause the application to hang indefinitely or fail silently on network errors, impacting user experience and reliability.

### Suggested Improvements
- Add timeout configuration for all API requests (client, extension, Gemini service)
- Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for key endpoints
- Add circuit breaker pattern for failing endpoints
- Update documentation to include API reliability best practices
- Add tests to simulate network failures and ensure graceful handling

### Relevant Code Files
- client/src/services/dashboardService.js
- client/src/utils/apiConfig.js
- extension/background.js
- server/services/geminiService.js

### Acceptance Criteria
- All API requests have timeout and retry logic

issues.title: Add Email Verification and Password Reset Features

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

issues.body:
### Problem
Currently, the application supports local registration and login, but lacks user email verification and password reset functionality. This can pose security risks and affect user experience. Email verification ensures users are genuine, and password reset is crucial for account recovery.

### Suggested Improvements
- Implement email verification for new local accounts. Send a verification link upon registration and restrict login until verified.
- Add password reset functionality allowing users to request a reset link to their email.
- Update authentication flows and documentation to reflect these changes.
- Add related backend and frontend tests.

### Acceptance Criteria
- New users must verify their email before accessing their account.
- Users can initiate password reset and set a new password via email.
- Documentation is updated with setup and usage instructions.
- Automated tests cover these new flows.

### Overview

We need to add a `CONTRIBUTING.md` file to clearly document how others can contribute to the project.

### Why It’s Important

- Encourages community contributions
- Sets expectations for code style, commit messages, and PR process
- Helps maintain consistency and quality as the project grows

### What to Include

- Project setup instructions (client and server)
- Coding conventions (folder structure, naming, etc.)
- Branch naming conventions (e.g., `feature/`, `bugfix/`)
- Commit message format (e.g., `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`)
- PR review process
- Issue creation guidelines
